text: Writing clean code is essential for any software project. The main goal is to make your code readable and easy to understand, not just for the computer, but for other developers. One important practice is following naming conventions: choose clear and descriptive names for your variables and functions, like `calculateTotal` instead of `ct`. Keep your functions small and focused on a single task. This makes them easier to test and reuse. Finally, remember that clean code improves maintainability. When your code is well-organized, it is much simpler to fix bugs or add new features later without breaking anything else.
